movies.title: Everytime
movies.review:
SFF #5
A real togs, togs, undies of a storyline. It was generally working until it the last 3rd or so where it took a surrealist turn that felt out of place and completely lost me (not that I really cared very much for the film before then). After that I was simply waiting for the film to end.

movies.title: Baby Jackfruit Baby Guava
movies.review:
SFF #4
Such a raw insight into the director’s own family dynamics. Complicated, funny and deeply personal, the evolution of each family member over the course of the documentary was something to behold.

movies.title: American Doctor
movies.review:
SFF #3
Incredible doco, so moving and (unsurprisingly) extremely hard to watch at times. Very important viewing.

movies.title: The Golden Spurtle
movies.review: Very cosy. Excellent sick day, in-bed comfort watch.

movies.title: The Cycle of Love
movies.review:
SFF# 2
First date: chai
Second date: meet the parents
Third date: 6,000 mile journey from India to Sweden.
So life affirming and inspiring. This movie gave me goosebumps - an absolute must see.
